content: Cushing syndrome results from chronic exposure to excess glucocorticoids. Etiologies: (1) Exogenous (most common) - iatrogenic from therapeutic glucocorticoid administration; (2) Endogenous ACTH-dependent (80-85%): Cushing disease (pituitary ACTH-secreting adenoma, ~70%), ectopic ACTH secretion (small cell lung cancer, bronchial carcinoids, other neuroendocrine tumors); (3) Endogenous ACTH-independent: adrenal adenoma/carcinoma, primary bilateral macronodular adrenal hyperplasia, primary pigmented nodular adrenocortical disease. Clinical features: central obesity, moon facies, dorsocervical fat pad (buffalo hump), proximal muscle weakness, wide purple striae (>1cm), easy bruising, osteoporosis, hypertension, glucose intolerance/diabetes, hirsutism, menstrual irregularities, mood changes. Complications: cardiovascular disease, infections, thromboembolism, fractures.
